signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike probability priced for today's meeting. RBNZ-led gains are holding at 0.588 despite Iran geopolitical risks. If RBNZ delivers the priced hike or maintains hawkish forward guidance, a crowded spec short unwind targets the 0.5950 level. NZD/USD at 0.588.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hawkish Shift + Vol Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1640 supported by hawkish ECB rhetoric ahead of the June 11 meeting. IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.2% generates a +2.9pt VRP, signaling elevated hedging demand for EUR upside protection. Despite the 175bp Fed-ECB carry disadvantage, the CESI net +5 beat score confirms improving Eurozone data momentum. Watch for a break of 1.1700 to confirm trend extension.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.38 remains bid on the 282bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage. JPY COT LF net short -81,624 represents a crowded carry trade, while the 25d RR at -1.41 (moderate put skew) confirms the options market is paying for downside protection against BoJ intervention. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 16.9 (normal volatility), but intervention risk is acute as price tests the 160.00 handle.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Retail Divergence + Macro Headwinds
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7141 faces pressure following softer Australian CPI data and a 2.60σ shock in the Unemployment Rate (4.5% vs 4.3% forecast). Retail sentiment is 70% short, diverging from the COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long). This institutional vs retail divergence suggests a potential long-liquidation phase if the RBA shifts to a neutral stance at the June 16 meeting.
